name: Arizona Pecan Chocolate Rum Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the salt with the flour and cut in the lard until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
2. In a small bowl, measure 1 tablespoon of the beaten eggs and set aside. Reserve the remaining eggs.
3. Add water and vinegar to the reserved eggs in the small bowl and stir.
4. Gradually incorporate the liquid into the flour mixture, stirring just until the dough holds together.
5. Lightly flour a work surface and rolling pin. Roll the dough into an 11-inch circle. Transfer it to a 9-inch pie pan and refrigerate if completing the pie later, or freeze if baking immediately.
6. Melt chocolate and butter in a heavy saucepan and let cool.
7. In another bowl, stir corn syrup and brown sugar with the reserved eggs. Add melted chocolate and butter, vanilla, rum, and chopped pecans; mix well.
8. Pour the filling into the pie shell.
9. Bake in a preheated oven for 1 hour, or until set. The filling may rise and then fall slightly. Remove from oven and cool on a rack.
10. Serve slightly warm or at room temperature.
